Tests the behavior of the modulus assignment operator with an object having custom valueOf and toString methods.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which override the default behavior of these methods in JavaScript. The `valueOf` method returns the number `1345`, and the `toString` method returns the string `"foo"`. The object is then assigned to a new variable `copyObject`, creating a reference to the same object. The code performs a modulus operation on `object` with `2` and uses an `assert` statement to check that the result of this operation is consistent with the result of the same operation on `copyObject`. This code provides narrow functionality, primarily demonstrating object method overriding and reference behavior in JavaScript.
